title: Strict Typing, Nullability, and Boundary Validation · Risk Brief: Type Tests
summary: Establish a strict type baseline without confusing static types with runtime validation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates type tests increment in a strict type and boundary contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test public type contracts and intentional compile failures.
Validate files, network data, environment variables, and user input at runtime.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Predict Type Tests
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates typing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates typing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Type Tests contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test public type contracts and intentional compile failures.

content.text: Test public type contracts and intentional compile failures. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern

content.text: Runtime unit tests cover type-level regressions. This fails the Type Tests contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Type Tests evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-type-tests-m4iacg: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Type Tests into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-type-tests-m4iacg:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Type Tests model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Type Tests applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test public type contracts and intentional compile failures.
